free_text: Excitation functions of alpha particle induced
reactions in cobalt in the energy range 10-120 MeV
-By authors.  It may be observed that our values are
smaller than those of Michel and Brinkman
(see REL-REF), in about half of the reactions studied,
by about 20-50% beyond the experimental errors.  The
reason for the discrepancy, to our belief, mainly lies
in different methods used for determining the incident
Spectroscopically pure cobalt foils of thickness 15.5
mg/cm2 were used as targets while aluminum foils of
varying thickness served as energy degraders.
Three  stacks were prepared to avoid the errors
in the energy due to particle straggling.  The first
stack had a thickness to degrade the alpha particle
energy from 120 to 75, the second from 75 to 35 and the
third from 45 to 10 MeV.  Each stack consists of a
series of cobalt foils, each sandwiched between a pair
of 4 mg/cm2 aluminum foils as catchers.  At the front
of each stack an aluminum foil of thickness 85 mg/cm2
Irradiation periods were about 1.5 H.  Keeping
in view the half lives and the yields, beam currents of
the order of 200 nA were maintained in each stack.
Several characteristic gamma rays were used for
the cross  section  calculation  of  each  reaction  to
provide an internal check on the consistency.
Including the statistical and other errors, the
overall projected error. The main sources of error in
The error in the detection efficiency
Error due to the monitor cross-sections
